stem: A rectangular swimming pool has a length of 20 feet and a width of 12 feet. A rectangular concrete deck surrounds the pool on all sides. If the deck extends 3 feet beyond the pool on each side, what is the area of the deck only (not including the pool area)?
explanation:
To find the area of the deck only, I need to find the total area (pool + deck) and subtract the pool area.

Pool dimensions: 20 ft × 12 ft
Pool area = 20 × 12 = 240 square feet

Since the deck extends 3 feet beyond the pool on each side, the total dimensions are:
Total length = 20 + 3 + 3 = 26 feet
Total width = 12 + 3 + 3 = 18 feet

Total area (pool + deck) = 26 × 18 = 468 square feet

Deck area only = Total area - Pool area
Deck area = 468 - 240 = 312 square feet

Therefore, the area of the deck only is 312 square feet.

distractorRationale.A: This results from calculating the perimeter of additional area (3 × 2 × (20 + 12)) instead of the actual deck area.
distractorRationale.B: This is the area of the pool itself, not the deck area.
distractorRationale.C: This is the correct answer: total area minus pool area.
distractorRationale.D: This is the total area including both pool and deck, not just the deck area.

imageDescription: A top-down view diagram showing a rectangular swimming pool (20 ft × 12 ft) centered within a larger rectangular deck. The deck extends 3 feet beyond the pool on all four sides. The pool should be shown in blue, and the deck area in gray. Dimensions should be clearly labeled: pool dimensions (20 ft × 12 ft) and the 3 ft extensions on each side, showing the total outer dimensions as 26 ft × 18 ft.
